product_risk_summary.intended_use: Digital healthcare platform for managing patient-practitioner encounters including appointment scheduling, clinical handovers, follow-ups, and care escalation
product_risk_summary.benefit_risk_conclusion: Acceptable - benefits of digital healthcare management outweigh residual risks

intended_use_and_context.intended_use: Platform24 Health Software is intended for use by healthcare providers and patients to manage clinical appointments, handovers, follow-ups, referrals, and care escalation in digital healthcare settings.

intended_users.role: Primary users managing patient care workflows
Book appointments, receive notifications, access care

contraindications: Complex clinical decisions requiring physical examination
intended_use_and_context.safety_class_rationale: Class B per IEC 62304 - software can contribute to hazardous situations but serious injury is unlikely

system_hazards.description: Unauthorized access to patient appointment and clinical data
system_hazards.hazardous_situation: Unauthorized party gains access to patient appointment information including symptoms, care history, and scheduling data
system_hazards.potential_harm: Privacy breach, patient confidentiality violation, regulatory non-compliance (GDPR, Patient Data Act)
system_hazards.harm_severity_rationale: Significant privacy impact and regulatory consequences, but no direct physical harm
system_hazards.probability_rationale: Multiple authentication layers, but attack surface exists via REST APIs

system_hazards.description: Patient appointment data corruption or loss
system_hazards.hazardous_situation: Appointment data becomes corrupted or lost due to software or infrastructure failure
system_hazards.potential_harm: Lost clinical context, treatment decisions based on incomplete information, care continuity disruption
system_hazards.harm_severity_rationale: Could impact treatment decisions though practitioners have other data sources
system_hazards.probability_rationale: Transaction management and database redundancy reduce likelihood

system_hazards.description: Handover to unavailable or incorrect practitioner
system_hazards.hazardous_situation: Patient appointment is handed over to unavailable practitioner or wrong specialty
system_hazards.potential_harm: Delayed patient care, inappropriate specialist assignment, patient confusion
system_hazards.harm_severity_rationale: Care continuity depends on correct handover; delays could worsen conditions
system_hazards.probability_rationale: State machine validation reduces likelihood, but edge cases possible

system_hazards.description: Critical appointment not escalated in time
system_hazards.hazardous_situation: Appointment requiring urgent attention is not escalated due to rule evaluation failure
system_hazards.potential_harm: Delayed emergency care, patient condition deterioration
system_hazards.harm_severity_rationale: Escalation failure could result in delayed response to deteriorating patient
system_hazards.probability_rationale: Default escalation on failure reduces probability

system_hazards.description: Follow-up notification not sent to patient
system_hazards.hazardous_situation: Patient does not receive scheduled follow-up notification
system_hazards.potential_harm: Patient misses follow-up appointment, condition may worsen without monitoring
system_hazards.harm_severity_rationale: Missed follow-up could delay necessary care, though patient can reschedule

system_hazards.description: Appointment scheduled at incorrect time due to timezone/serialization error
system_hazards.hazardous_situation: Appointment time displayed or stored incorrectly due to timezone handling
system_hazards.potential_harm: Patient misses appointment, minor care delay
system_hazards.harm_severity_rationale: Appointment can be rescheduled, temporary inconvenience
system_hazards.probability_rationale: JSR-310 date handling with explicit timezone reduces issues

system_hazards.description: JMS message loss causing appointment data inconsistency
system_hazards.hazardous_situation: Booking data becomes inconsistent between services due to message loss
system_hazards.harm_severity_rationale: Scheduling conflicts disrupt care but can be manually resolved
system_hazards.probability_rationale: DLQ and message persistence reduce message loss

software_controls.implementation: Spring Security with JWT validation, role-based access via @PreAuthorize

software_controls.description: Database transaction management with automatic rollback
Optimistic locking for concurrent modification detection

software_controls.implementation: State transition validation before handover operations

software_controls.description: Escalation rule validation with default escalation on failure
software_controls.implementation: Rule evaluation logging, default escalation when rules engine fails

software_controls.description: ShedLock distributed locking for scheduled tasks
software_controls.implementation: Database-backed distributed lock for exactly-once execution

software_controls.description: JSR-310 date/time handling with explicit timezone
JMS dead letter queue for failed message processing
software_controls.implementation: Artemis DLQ configuration, message retry with backoff

software_controls.description: Clinical audit logging for all patient data access
software_controls.implementation: AuditLoggingService captures all clinical data operations

non_software_controls.implementation_requirement: Training program for clinical handover procedures
Clustered JMS broker with message persistence

residual_risk_evaluation.justification: JWT authentication plus audit logging reduces unauthorized access to rare occurrence. Detection capability enables response.
Transaction management with optimistic locking and audit trail makes data corruption very rare and detectable.
State machine validation prevents invalid transitions. Training addresses human factors. Optimistic locking prevents concurrent modification.
Default escalation on failure ensures critical cases are flagged. Monitoring enables rapid response to escalation system issues. Residual risk accepted given safety net approach.
Distributed locking ensures scheduled jobs run exactly once in cluster. Patients can reschedule if needed.
Explicit timezone handling in JSR-310 effectively eliminates timezone serialization issues.
DLQ and HA broker configuration make message loss rare. Manual reconciliation process available for edge cases.

overall_residual_risk_evaluation.high_risk_justification: SYS-P24-HAZ-004 (escalation failure) has medium residual risk due to critical severity. Accepted because: (1) default escalation on failure provides safety net, (2) monitoring alerts enable rapid response, (3) clinical staff trained to manually escalate concerning cases.
overall_residual_risk_evaluation.benefit_risk_analysis: Platform24 enables timely access to healthcare, reduces administrative burden on providers, and improves care coordination. These benefits significantly outweigh the controlled residual risks.
post_market_commitments: Continuous monitoring of escalation system performance
User feedback analysis for safety signals

conclusions: All identified hazards have appropriate controls
Post-market surveillance plan addresses monitoring needs
